agent_output:
```json
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A global logistics platform adding a driver-facing earnings transparency feature across three jurisdictions with materially different labor classifications, legal requirements, and institutional approval processes. The feature must display base pay, per-delivery bonus, tips, and deductions in real time.",
    "bounded_context": "Driver Compensation / Earnings Visibility",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Base Pay", "definition": "The fixed or hourly compensation a driver receives for being available or completing a shift, independent of individual deliveries. For employees (CA, DE), this maps to a wage; for contractors (ID), this maps to a base service fee." },
      { "term": "Per-Delivery Bonus", "definition": "Variable compensation tied to completion of an individual delivery. May include surge/demand multipliers." },
      { "term": "Tip", "definition": "Voluntary gratuity from a customer directed to the driver. In California, tips must never be counted toward minimum wage obligations (Labor Code §351)." },
      { "term": "Deduction", "definition": "Any amount subtracted from gross earnings. For employees: tax withholding, benefits, garnishments. For contractors: platform commission, equipment fees, service charges. The composition is jurisdiction-dependent." },
      { "term": "Earnings Breakdown", "definition": "An itemized view of all compensation components and deductions for a given period or delivery, presented to the driver." },
      { "term": "Wage Statement", "definition": "California-specific legal term (Labor Code §226): a mandatory itemized statement showing gross wages, total hours, piece rates, deductions, net wages, pay period, and employer identity." },
      { "term": "Works Council (Betriebsrat)", "definition": "A German employee representative body with co-determination rights over changes to compensation monitoring and display systems under the Works Constitution Act (BetrVG §87)." },
      { "term": "Driver Classification", "definition": "The legal relationship between driver and platform: 'employee' (CA under AB5, DE) or 'independent contractor' (ID). Determines applicable labor law, deduction types, and transparency obligations." },
      { "term": "Real-Time Earnings", "definition": "Earnings data updated and visible to the driver with minimal delay after the compensable event occurs. Spec does not define latency tolerance — see escalations." },
      { "term": "Net Pay", "definition": "The final amount a driver receives after all deductions are applied to gross earnings." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "California drivers MUST receive an itemized earnings breakdown that satisfies Labor Code §226 wage statement requirements: gross wages, total hours worked, piece-rate units (deliveries), all deductions itemized, net wages, pay period dates, and employer name/address.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Failure to provide compliant wage statements exposes the platform to statutory penalties of $50-$100 per pay period per employee, plus potential class action liability. This is a legal compliance constraint, not a policy choice.",
      "testable_assertion": "Every earnings breakdown displayed to a California-classified driver includes all nine fields required by Labor Code §226."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "The earnings transparency feature MUST NOT be rolled out to German drivers until written Betriebsrat approval is obtained and recorded in the system.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Deploying compensation display changes without works council consent violates BetrVG §87(1)(6) co-determination rights. The works council can obtain an injunction halting the feature and the platform faces legal sanctions. This is a hard deployment gate, not a soft preference.",
      "testable_assertion": "The system contains a jurisdiction-level feature gate for Germany that is OFF by default and can only be enabled when a Betriebsrat approval record exists with a valid approval date."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Tips MUST be displayed as a separate line item and MUST NOT be commingled with base pay or bonuses in any jurisdiction.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "In California, commingling tips with wages violates Labor Code §351 and creates wage theft liability. Applying this universally prevents jurisdiction-specific display bugs and aligns with the transparency intent of the feature.",
      "testable_assertion": "In every rendered earnings breakdown, tips appear as a distinct, labeled line item separate from base pay and per-delivery bonus."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Deductions shown MUST be itemized individually (e.g., 'Federal Tax Withholding', 'Platform Commission', 'Equipment Rental') — never as a single aggregated 'Total Deductions' line.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Itemization is the purpose of the transparency feature and is legally required in California, but the granularity level is a policy decision that could be adjusted per jurisdiction.",
      "testable_assertion": "Each deduction in the earnings breakdown has a distinct label and amount; no generic 'deductions' aggregate appears without its constituent items."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "The earnings breakdown MUST reflect the driver's legal classification in that jurisdiction: employee-type fields (hours worked, hourly rate, tax withholding) for CA/DE; contractor-type fields (service fees, platform commission) for ID.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Displaying employee-style wage data to contractors implies an employment relationship and creates misclassification liability. Displaying contractor-style data to employees omits legally required information. The data model must be classification-aware.",
      "testable_assertion": "A California or Germany driver's breakdown includes hours worked and tax withholding; an Indonesia driver's breakdown includes platform commission and does not include hours worked or tax withholding."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Displayed earnings MUST exactly match the platform's payment/payroll ledger — no rounding, estimation, or projection of unfinalized amounts.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Showing a number that differs from what the driver is actually paid is materially misleading and creates legal exposure in all jurisdictions. Wage statement accuracy is a hard legal requirement in CA.",
      "testable_assertion": "For any completed delivery, the sum of (base pay + bonus + tips - deductions) displayed equals the net payment recorded in the payment ledger to the cent."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "Earnings data MUST be displayed in the local currency of the driver's operating jurisdiction (USD for CA, EUR for DE, IDR for ID).",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Displaying earnings in a foreign currency is misleading and non-compliant with local wage statement laws. A driver's pay must be denominated in the currency they are paid in.",
      "testable_assertion": "A California driver sees amounts in USD, a Germany driver in EUR, an Indonesia driver in IDR, with correct currency symbols and formatting."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "If deductions for a period exceed gross earnings, the breakdown MUST show the negative net balance explicitly rather than hiding or clamping to zero.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Transparency requires showing the real number. However, whether the platform allows negative balances to carry forward or writes them off is a business policy decision.",
      "testable_assertion": "When deductions exceed gross earnings, the net pay line displays a negative value with clear visual indication."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "A driver classified as an employee in California completes a delivery",
      "when": "They view their earnings breakdown",
      "then": "They see an itemized statement with base pay (hourly rate × hours), per-delivery bonus, tips (separate line), each deduction itemized (tax withholding, benefits, etc.), net pay, pay period dates, and employer identity — compliant with Labor Code §226"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-2",
      "given": "The Betriebsrat has NOT approved the earnings transparency feature for Germany",
      "when": "A German driver attempts to access the earnings breakdown",
      "then": "The feature is not available; the driver sees no earnings breakdown UI and no partial or placeholder data"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-3",
      "given": "The Betriebsrat HAS approved the feature and the approval is recorded in the system",
      "when": "A German driver views their earnings breakdown",
      "then": "They see a fully itemized breakdown with employee-appropriate fields in EUR"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-4",
      "given": "An independent contractor driver in Indonesia completes a delivery",
      "when": "They view their earnings breakdown",
      "then": "They see base service fee, per-delivery bonus, tips (separate line), platform commission, and net payout in IDR — with no employee-specific fields like hours worked or tax withholding"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-5",
      "given": "A driver has completed 3 deliveries today with varying bonuses and a tip adjustment (reversal) on delivery #2",
      "when": "They view their real-time earnings",
      "then": "Each delivery shows its own breakdown, the tip reversal is reflected accurately, and the running total matches the sum of all individual delivery net amounts"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-6",
      "given": "A delivery is completed but the final tip amount is not yet finalized (customer has a tipping window)",
      "when": "The driver views earnings for that delivery",
      "then": "The tip field shows a pending/unfinalized state rather than $0 or an estimated amount, and net pay is calculated excluding the pending tip"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-7",
      "given": "Any driver in any jurisdiction views their earnings breakdown",
      "when": "They compare the displayed net pay to their actual bank deposit or wallet credit",
      "then": "The amounts match exactly — no discrepancy due to rounding, timing, or display error"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "A delivery is canceled mid-transit after partial completion", "expected_behavior": "The earnings breakdown shows the cancellation and any partial pay or cancellation fee per jurisdiction policy. No phantom earnings appear." },
    { "scenario": "Customer adjusts or removes tip after initial tip is shown to driver", "expected_behavior": "The earnings breakdown updates to reflect the new tip amount. The change is visible in an audit trail or history view so the driver understands why their total changed." },
    { "scenario": "Multiple deduction types reduce net pay below zero for a period", "expected_behavior": "Negative net balance is displayed transparently. The system does not hide or zero-clamp earnings." },
    { "scenario": "Platform applies a new deduction type (e.g., equipment fee) not previously categorized", "expected_behavior": "The deduction appears as a new labeled line item. It must never be silently folded into an existing deduction category." },
    { "scenario": "Driver operates near a jurisdiction boundary (e.g., delivers across California state line)", "expected_behavior": "Earnings for each delivery are classified under the jurisdiction where the work was performed. The breakdown reflects the correct classification and rules for that jurisdiction." },
    { "scenario": "German Betriebsrat approval is revoked after feature was already live", "expected_behavior": "The feature is immediately disabled for German drivers. The feature gate returns to OFF. Historical data remains accessible only if legally required." },
    { "scenario": "System experiences a delay in payment ledger sync while driver views 'real-time' earnings", "expected_behavior": "The UI shows the most recent confirmed data with a clear timestamp. It does NOT show stale data as current or fabricate projected amounts." },
    { "scenario": "Indonesian contractor receives a government-mandated bonus (e.g., THR holiday allowance)", "expected_behavior": "The bonus appears as a separate labeled line item appropriate to contractor classification — not as 'wages' or 'salary'." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Non-compliance with California Labor Code §226 exposes the platform to $50-$100 per violation per pay period, scalable to class-action liability across all CA drivers. Germany non-compliance with BetrVG can result in injunctions halting the feature. Correct implementation builds driver trust, potentially reducing churn and recruitment costs (typically $1,000-$3,000 per driver).",
    "operational": "Requires jurisdiction-aware earnings rendering pipeline, a Betriebsrat approval workflow and feature gate for Germany, and classification-driven data models. Ongoing maintenance includes monitoring legislative changes across three jurisdictions and extending the framework as new markets are added.",
    "user": "Drivers gain visibility into exactly how their pay is calculated, reducing pay-related support tickets (industry benchmarks suggest 20-40% reduction). Transparency builds trust and can improve driver satisfaction and retention. Incorrect or misleading data has the opposite effect — erodes trust rapidly.",
    "risk": "PRIMARY RISK: Misclassification signaling — showing employee-style breakdowns to contractors or vice versa could be used as evidence in classification lawsuits. SECONDARY RISK: Rolling out to Germany without Betriebsrat approval. TERTIARY RISK: 'Real-time' display showing unfinalized or inconsistent data that drivers screenshot and use in disputes.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Drivers (California)", "impact": "Legally entitled to this information. Feature is a compliance requirement, not a perk. Must meet wage statement standards." },
      { "group": "Drivers (Germany)", "impact": "Cannot access feature until Betriebsrat approves. Premature rollout could trigger works council conflict and labor relations damage." },
      { "group": "Drivers (Indonesia)", "impact": "Receive a transparency benefit with no legal mandate. Must not receive employee-framed data that implies reclassification." },
      { "group": "Works Council (Germany)", "impact": "Has co-determination right. Must be engaged before design is finalized, not just before launch — they may require changes to the data displayed." },
      { "group": "Legal & Compliance Team", "impact": "Must validate wage statement compliance for CA, approve contractor display for ID, and manage Betriebsrat engagement for DE." },
      { "group": "Finance / Payroll", "impact": "Earnings display must be sourced from the canonical payroll/payment ledger. Any discrepancy is a defect." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Will receive driver inquiries about displayed earnings. Needs training on what each line item means per jurisdiction." },
      { "group": "Product / Engineering", "impact": "Must build a jurisdiction-aware, classification-aware rendering system — not a one-size-fits-all UI." }
    ]
  },
```
